{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.1": 2, "RX.1": 1, "RX.3": 3, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The critique identifies a major framing contradiction and several evidence/scoping issues, but doesn't offer a clear reframing of the core concept.",
    "RX.1": "The critique mentions uncertainty about the confidence token but doesn't explicitly score it on a scale.",
    "RX.3": "The critique points out some scoping issues with the dataset and prior work positioning, suggesting a partial adherence to the requested scope.",
    "RX.4": "The critique does not identify any harmful content or regressions in the output.",
    "RX.5": "The critique mentions lack of traceability for most edits but doesn't score it on a scale."
  },
  "confidence": {"R1.1": 0.9, "RX.1": 0.6, "RX.3": 0.8, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.7},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The critique identifies a major framing contradiction and several evidence/scoping issues, but doesn't offer a clear reframing of the core concept.
rationale.RX.1: The critique mentions uncertainty about the confidence token but doesn't explicitly score it on a scale.
rationale.RX.3: The critique points out some scoping issues with the dataset and prior work positioning, suggesting a partial adherence to the requested scope.
rationale.RX.4: The critique does not identify any harmful content or regressions in the output.
rationale.RX.5: The critique mentions lack of traceability for most edits but doesn't score it on a scale.
